name: Legend Diver
description: The Longines Legend Diver pays homage to the brand's pioneering dive watches from the 1960s. Its distinctive super-compressor-style case and internal rotating bezel make it a faithful reinterpretation of the original. Powered by the L888.5 automatic calibre with silicon hairspring, it blends vintage charm with modern reliability.

name: Spirit
description: The Longines Spirit collection celebrates the brand's heritage with pioneering aviators and explorers. This 42mm model features a COSC-certified automatic movement with silicon hairspring and comes with an interchangeable strap system. The collection was introduced in 2020 and quickly became a cornerstone of the Longines range.

name: HydroConquest
description: The Longines HydroConquest is a versatile sport diver built for both professional and recreational use. Introduced as a line in 2007 and continually refined, the current generation features the L888.5 calibre with silicon hairspring and a ceramic bezel insert. It offers 300m water resistance at an accessible price point.

name: Master Collection
description: The Longines Master Collection represents the brand's mastery of traditional watchmaking with classic, refined design. This model features a moon phase complication and a barleycorn-textured dial. Powered by the L899 calibre based on the ETA A31.L81, it offers sophisticated complications in an elegant 40mm case.

name: Conquest VHP
description: The Longines Conquest VHP (Very High Precision) is among the most accurate quartz watches on the market, with a deviation of just +/- 5 seconds per year. The GPD (Gear Position Detection) system automatically resets hands after impact or magnetic field exposure. It offers a perpetual calendar that requires no adjustment until 2399.

name: Heritage Classic
description: The Longines Heritage Classic is inspired by archival designs from the 1930s, featuring a distinctive sector dial with tachymeter scale. The 38.5mm case size stays true to vintage proportions, while the modern L893.5 calibre with silicon hairspring ensures contemporary performance. It captures the elegance of a bygone era.

name: Dolce Vita
description: The Longines DolceVita evokes the refined lifestyle of la dolce vita with its elegant rectangular case. This ladies' model features a diamond-set bezel and mother-of-pearl dial, embodying Longines' tradition of creating sophisticated dress watches. The quartz movement ensures reliable precision in a compact, graceful form.

name: Record
description: The Longines Record collection represents the brand's first COSC-certified automatic line. Featuring the L888.4 calibre with silicon hairspring, this model delivers chronometric precision in a classically designed 40mm case. The annual calendar complication and refined dial make it a compelling entry-level COSC-certified timepiece.

name: Spirit Zulu Time
description: The Longines Spirit Zulu Time is the brand's first modern GMT watch, introduced in 2022. Featuring a true caller-GMT movement (Calibre L844.4) with an independently adjustable local hour hand, it allows tracking three time zones simultaneously. The COSC-certified calibre with silicon hairspring delivers 72 hours of power reserve.

name: Ultra-Chron
description: The Longines Ultra-Chron revives the legendary 1968 high-frequency timepiece that was among the most accurate mechanical watches of its day. The modern reissue runs at 36,000 vph using the exclusive Calibre L836.6, delivering chronometric precision. Its cushion-shaped case and retro-inspired dial honor the original's pioneering spirit.

name: Flagship Heritage
description: The Longines Flagship Heritage draws on the Flagship line introduced in 1957, one of the brand's most enduring collections. This 38.5mm model features a clean, minimalist dial with a date window and crosshair pattern. The L888.5 calibre with silicon hairspring offers 72 hours of power reserve in a refined, dress-watch package.

name: Seastar 1000 Powermatic 80
description: The Tissot Seastar 1000 is a robust dive watch offering 300m water resistance and a unidirectional ceramic bezel at an accessible price. Powered by the Powermatic 80 movement with 80 hours of power reserve and a silicon hairspring, it represents exceptional value in the Swiss dive watch category. The 43mm case features a helium escape valve.

name: PRX Powermatic 80
description: The Tissot PRX Powermatic 80 revives the 1978 original with its distinctive integrated bracelet design and clean dial. Reintroduced in 2021, the PRX became a viral sensation and one of the most popular watches in its price range. The Powermatic 80 movement provides 80 hours of power reserve with a silicon hairspring for improved accuracy.

name: Gentleman Powermatic 80 Silicium
description: The Tissot Gentleman is a versatile daily-wear watch offering refined styling and impressive specs for its price. The Powermatic 80 Silicium movement delivers 80 hours of power reserve and enhanced magnetic resistance thanks to the silicon balance spring. Its 40mm case and tapering bracelet give it dressy proportions that work with any attire.

name: T-Touch Connect Solar
description: The Tissot T-Touch Connect Solar combines Swiss-made craftsmanship with smartwatch functionality. Solar-powered with up to six months of battery life in connected mode, it features a touch-sensitive sapphire crystal for accessing functions like compass, altimeter, and barometer. It runs a low-power Swiss OS rather than Wear OS.

name: Heritage Visodate
description: The Tissot Heritage Visodate recalls the elegance of the original 1950s Visodate with its clean dial, day-date display, and vintage-inspired styling. The name 'Visodate' referred to the visible date window, an innovation at the time. This modern reinterpretation uses an automatic movement and remains one of Tissot's most popular dress watches.

name: Le Locle Powermatic 80
description: Named after the Swiss town where Tissot was founded in 1853, the Le Locle Powermatic 80 is a refined dress watch with classic proportions. Its textured Jura-pattern dial pays tribute to the Jura mountains that surround the brand's hometown. The 39.3mm case houses the reliable Powermatic 80 calibre.

name: Chemin des Tourelles Powermatic 80
description: The Tissot Chemin des Tourelles is named after the street address of Tissot's headquarters and factory in Le Locle. This model features a more contemporary, slightly dressier design with refined indexes and a 42mm case. The Powermatic 80 movement delivers an extended three-day-plus power reserve with silicon hairspring technology.

name: PRX
description: The quartz-powered Tissot PRX offers the same iconic 1970s-inspired integrated bracelet design as its automatic sibling at a lower price point. The 40mm case with waffle-textured dial made this model a breakout hit when it launched in 2021. Its accessible pricing made it a gateway watch for many enthusiasts entering the hobby.

name: Supersport Chrono
description: The Tissot Supersport Chrono is a sporty quartz chronograph designed for active lifestyles. Its 45.5mm case gives it a commanding wrist presence, while the reliable Swiss quartz movement ensures hassle-free timekeeping. As the official timekeeper of numerous sporting events, Tissot brings its timing expertise to this affordable chronograph.

name: Ballade Powermatic 80 COSC
description: The Tissot Ballade is the brand's first collection to feature a COSC-certified Powermatic 80 movement with silicon balance spring. Offering chronometric precision that rivals watches at much higher price points, the Ballade represents Tissot's most advanced movement technology in an elegant 41mm dress watch format.

name: Promaster Diver
description: The Citizen Promaster Diver BN0151 is widely regarded as one of the best value dive watches available. Powered by Citizen's Eco-Drive solar technology, it never needs a battery change. With 200m water resistance, a unidirectional bezel, and ISO 6425 certification, it delivers genuine dive watch capability at a fraction of the competition's price.

name: Eco-Drive Chronograph
description: The Citizen Eco-Drive Chronograph combines solar-powered convenience with sporty chronograph functionality. Its Eco-Drive technology converts any light source into energy, eliminating the need for battery changes. The 43mm stainless steel case houses a reliable quartz chronograph movement with 1/5-second precision.

name: Promaster Navihawk A-T
description: The Citizen Promaster Navihawk A-T is a feature-packed aviation watch with atomic timekeeping via multi-band radio wave reception. It synchronizes with atomic clocks in five global stations for unmatched accuracy. The Eco-Drive solar movement powers an impressive array of functions including world time, chronograph, and a slide-rule bezel for aviation calculations.

name: Tsuyosa
description: The Citizen Tsuyosa (meaning 'strength' in Japanese) is a colorful automatic watch that became a social media sensation upon its 2023 release. Offering a Miyota 8210 automatic movement, sapphire crystal, and vibrant dial colors at under $350, it directly competes with the Tissot PRX. Its retro-modern design and integrated-style bracelet make it a compelling everyday watch.

name: Series 8
description: The Citizen Series 8 represents the brand's premium mechanical line, positioned just below The Citizen. This model features Citizen's in-house Calibre 0950 automatic movement with a high 28,800 vph frequency and anti-magnetic properties. The refined 40mm case with integrated bracelet offers a sophisticated alternative to Swiss competitors at a fraction of the price.

name: Satellite Wave GPS F950
description: The Citizen Satellite Wave GPS F950 connects to GPS satellites anywhere on Earth for world's-fastest GPS time acquisition at just 3 seconds. The Eco-Drive movement is solar-powered and automatically adjusts time zones and daylight saving time. With titanium Super Titanium case treatment and a perpetual calendar, it represents the pinnacle of Citizen's technology.

name: Promaster Tough
description: The Citizen Promaster Tough is built for extreme conditions with a Super Titanium case that is five times harder than stainless steel and 40% lighter. The Eco-Drive solar movement ensures it never needs a battery. With 200m water resistance and shock-resistant construction, it is designed for adventurers who demand rugged reliability.

name: Eco-Drive One
description: The Citizen Eco-Drive One is the world's thinnest light-powered watch at just 2.98mm thick, a stunning feat of engineering. Its ultra-thin Calibre 8826 is only 1mm thick and converts even the faintest light into energy. The cermet (ceramic-metal composite) case and sapphire crystal create a dress watch of extraordinary refinement and technological achievement.

name: Corso
description: The Citizen Corso is a versatile Eco-Drive dress watch offering clean lines and everyday wearability. Its solar-powered movement requires no battery changes, while the stainless steel case and bracelet provide durability for daily wear. The Corso line represents Citizen's accessible everyday collection with reliable Eco-Drive technology.

name: Promaster Aqualand
description: The Citizen Promaster Aqualand features a built-in analog depth gauge that mechanically measures diving depth up to 70 meters. First introduced in 1985 as the world's first dive watch with an electronic depth sensor, the modern version continues this legacy with Eco-Drive solar power and ISO 6425 certification for 200m water resistance.

name: F-91W Classic Digital
description: The Casio F-91W is one of the most iconic and best-selling watches in history, produced continuously since 1989. Its combination of extreme affordability, reliability, and lightweight resin construction has made it ubiquitous worldwide. Despite its modest price, it offers an LED backlight, stopwatch, daily alarm, and water resistance.

name: A168WA Vintage
description: The Casio A168WA is a retro-styled digital watch with a stainless steel bracelet that has become a fashion staple. Building on the original A158 from 1978, this model adds an electro-luminescent backlight for improved visibility. Its classic gold-and-silver-toned design has made it a favorite among both vintage watch enthusiasts and fashion-forward consumers.

name: MRW-200H
description: The Casio MRW-200H is a budget analog watch styled after a dive watch, offering a unidirectional rotating bezel and 100m water resistance at an incredibly low price. Its quartz movement provides reliable timekeeping, and the large 44.6mm resin case is both lightweight and durable. It has become a popular choice for those seeking a capable beater watch.

name: Edifice EFR-S108D
description: The Casio Edifice EFR-S108D features a slim sapphire crystal and a thin-profile case, positioning it as a refined option within the Edifice line. The collection is inspired by motorsport and mechanical engineering aesthetics. This model offers a chronograph function, date display, and 100m water resistance in an accessible stainless steel package.

name: Oceanus OCW-T200S
description: The Casio Oceanus OCW-T200S is a premium solar-powered titanium watch with multi-band 6 atomic timekeeping and Bluetooth smartphone connectivity. The Oceanus line represents Casio's most refined analog collection, combining advanced technology with elegant design. Its slim titanium case and sapphire crystal make it a sophisticated daily wearer with unmatched accuracy.

name: Pro Trek PRW-6900Y
description: The Casio Pro Trek PRW-6900Y is an outdoor-oriented digital-analog watch featuring the Triple Sensor (altimeter/barometer, compass, thermometer), Tough Solar power, and Multi-Band 6 atomic timekeeping. The biomass plastic case demonstrates Casio's commitment to sustainability. Designed for mountaineers, hikers, and outdoor enthusiasts who need reliable environmental data.

name: Duro
description: The Casio Duro MDV-106 has earned the nickname 'Marlin' and gained cult status as arguably the best dive-style watch under $50. With 200m water resistance, a screw-down crown, and a rotating bezel, it offers genuine dive watch specifications at a price that defies belief. Bill Gates was famously photographed wearing one, further cementing its legend.

specs.caseMaterial: Resin case with stainless steel back

name: World Time
description: The Casio AE-1200WH, often called the 'Casio Royale' for its resemblance to the watch worn by James Bond in early films, is a digital world time watch covering 31 time zones. Its retro design with a world map display and multiple function buttons offers an impressive feature set including 5 alarms, a countdown timer, and a stopwatch at an entry-level price.

name: GA-2100 CasiOak
description: The Casio GA-2100, nicknamed 'CasiOak' by enthusiasts for its resemblance to the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, became one of the most sought-after G-Shock models upon its 2019 release. Its carbon core guard structure allows for a thin 11.8mm profile while maintaining G-Shock toughness. The octagonal bezel design and clean dial set it apart from typical G-Shocks.

name: W-800H
description: The Casio W-800H is a step up from the F-91W, offering 100m water resistance and a 10-year battery life. Its digital display features a clear day-date readout with a dual time function. For under $20, it provides robust water resistance and long battery life, making it one of the best ultra-budget digital watches available.

name: Classic Square
description: The G-Shock DW-5600E is the direct descendant of the original 1983 DW-5000C that started the G-Shock revolution. Its iconic square case design has remained largely unchanged for four decades, becoming one of the most recognizable watch silhouettes in the world. The Triple 10 design philosophy promises 10m drop resistance, 10 bar water resistance, and 10-year battery life.

name: CasiOak
description: The G-Shock GA-2100-1A1, nicknamed 'CasiOak' for its octagonal bezel reminiscent of the Royal Oak, became a viral sensation and one of G-Shock's best-selling models. Its Carbon Core Guard structure achieves a remarkably thin 11.8mm profile for a G-Shock. The analog-digital display with double LED light provides both style and functionality.

name: Multiband 6 Square
description: The G-Shock GW-M5610U upgrades the classic square design with Tough Solar power and Multi-Band 6 atomic timekeeping. It syncs with six global atomic clock stations for razor-sharp accuracy and never needs a battery change. The 'U' revision added a new module with improved features including a full auto-LED and selectable illumination duration.

name: Full Metal Square
description: The G-Shock GMW-B5000D is a full stainless steel reinterpretation of the original 1983 G-Shock square, released for the 35th anniversary in 2018. The metal case and bracelet are treated with DLC coating and feature Tough Solar, Multi-Band 6, and Bluetooth connectivity. It maintains G-Shock's shock resistance through an internal module suspension system.

name: CasiOak Bluetooth
description: The G-Shock GA-B2100 adds Tough Solar power and Bluetooth smartphone connectivity to the beloved CasiOak design. The Bluetooth link enables automatic time adjustment, easy world time setting, and phone-finding features via the Casio Watches app. It retains the slim Carbon Core Guard case and octagonal bezel that made the GA-2100 an icon.

name: G-Shock Move
description: The G-Shock GBD-200 is a fitness-oriented G-Shock with step counting, Bluetooth connectivity for GPS-linked running data, and a vibration alert. Its slim square design recalls classic G-Shock lines while adding modern activity tracking features. The MIP (Memory in Pixel) LCD display offers excellent visibility in direct sunlight.

name: Rangeman
description: The G-Shock Rangeman GW-9400 is designed for survival situations, featuring the Triple Sensor (altimeter/barometer, compass, thermometer) with Tough Solar and Multi-Band 6. Its name evokes the U.S. Army Rangers, and its rugged design is built for the most extreme environments. The large buttons can be operated with gloves, and the display shows sensor data clearly.

name: Frogman
description: The G-Shock Frogman GWF-A1000 is the first analog Frogman in the series' history, designed specifically for divers and marine professionals. Since 1993, the Frogman has been G-Shock's premier dive watch with ISO 200m water resistance and an asymmetric case design. This generation adds a carbon monocoque case and Bluetooth-linked dive log capability.

name: G-Steel
description: The G-Shock GST-B400D is the thinnest G-STEEL ever produced at just 12.9mm, thanks to a new Tough Solar-powered module. The stainless steel bezel adds premium materials to the shock-resistant resin case, while Bluetooth connectivity enables automatic time syncing and easy world time adjustments. The dual-dial design blends sport with sophistication.

specs.caseMaterial: Stainless steel bezel with resin case

name: MR-G
description: The G-Shock MR-G represents the absolute pinnacle of the G-Shock lineup. Handcrafted in Japan using full titanium with deep-hardened DLC coating, it combines traditional Japanese craftsmanship with cutting-edge technology. The MR-G line was introduced in 1996 as the luxury tier of G-Shock, and this model features GPS timekeeping, Bluetooth, and Tough Solar in a premium package.

name: DW-6900 Classic
description: The G-Shock DW-6900 is one of the most recognizable models in the G-Shock family, beloved for its distinctive triple-eye display and front-facing EL backlight button. First released in 1995, it became a hip-hop and streetwear icon in the 1990s and 2000s. The BB-1 blacked-out variant offers a stealthy monochrome look with a reverse LCD display.

name: Khaki Field Mechanical
description: The Hamilton Khaki Field Mechanical is a direct descendant of the military watches Hamilton produced for U.S. forces during World War II. This 38mm model features the hand-wound H-50 calibre with an impressive 80-hour power reserve, far exceeding typical manual-wind movements. Its clean military dial with full luminous Arabic numerals stays true to its martial heritage.

name: Khaki Field Auto 38mm
description: The Hamilton Khaki Field Auto 38mm is considered one of the best value automatic field watches available. Its 38mm case wears perfectly on most wrists, while the H-10 calibre with 80-hour power reserve ensures reliability far exceeding its price point. Hamilton's deep military heritage gives this watch genuine provenance that few competitors can match.

name: Jazzmaster Open Heart
description: The Hamilton Jazzmaster Open Heart showcases the beauty of mechanical watchmaking through a cutaway dial that reveals the balance wheel and escapement. The Jazzmaster collection draws inspiration from Hamilton's American roots and the improvisational spirit of jazz music. The H-10 calibre with 80-hour power reserve is visible through both the open dial and exhibition caseback.

name: Ventura Auto
description: The Hamilton Ventura is the world's first electric watch, originally designed in 1957 by renowned industrial designer Richard Arbib. Its distinctive asymmetric shield-shaped case became iconic when worn by Elvis Presley in 'Blue Hawaii' (1961) and later appeared in 'Men in Black.' This automatic version preserves the adventurous design with modern H-10 movement technology.

name: Khaki Aviation Pilot Day Date
description: The Hamilton Khaki Aviation Pilot Day Date draws from Hamilton's long history as a supplier of aviation instruments, including chronometers used on early commercial flights and military aircraft. The 42mm case features a clean aviator-style dial with day and date displays. Hamilton's connection to aviation dates back to 1918, when it began supplying watches to U.S. airmail pilots.

name: Khaki Navy Scuba Auto
description: The Hamilton Khaki Navy Scuba Auto is a capable dive watch offering 100m water resistance with a unidirectional rotating bezel. The 40mm case with the H-10 automatic calibre delivering 80 hours of power reserve makes it an excellent everyday sport watch. Hamilton's naval heritage traces back to marine chronometers used by the U.S. Navy.

name: American Classic PSR
description: The Hamilton PSR is a faithful recreation of the Pulsar, the world's first digital watch, originally created by Hamilton in 1970. The modern PSR features a hybrid OLED and LCD display that mimics the red LED display of the original Pulsar. Pressing the button illuminates the OLED display, recreating the iconic experience of the original space-age timepiece.

name: Intra-Matic Auto Chrono
description: The Hamilton Intra-Matic Auto Chrono is a vintage-inspired chronograph that recalls the brand's 1968 Chrono-Matic. The 40mm case houses the H-31 automatic chronograph calibre with 60-hour power reserve, based on the Valjoux 7753. Its panda dial with contrasting sub-registers and domed sapphire crystal evoke the golden era of chronograph design.

name: Jazzmaster Performer Auto
description: The Hamilton Jazzmaster Performer Auto represents a modern, versatile take on the Jazzmaster collection. Featuring a 38mm case that suits a wide range of wrists, it houses the H-10 automatic calibre with 80 hours of power reserve. The refined dial with applied hour markers and a date window creates a watch equally at home in the office or on the weekend.

name: Khaki Field Titanium Auto
description: The Hamilton Khaki Field Titanium Auto combines the classic field watch formula with a lightweight titanium case. At 42mm with the H-10 calibre delivering 80 hours of power reserve, it offers the same proven reliability as the steel versions in a significantly lighter package. The titanium construction makes it an excellent choice for extended outdoor use.

name: True Square Automatic
description: The Rado True Square features the brand's signature high-tech ceramic in a distinctive square case, combining avant-garde design with advanced materials science. Rado pioneered the use of high-tech ceramic in watchmaking, and the True Square showcases this expertise with a case that is virtually scratch-proof, lightweight, and hypoallergenic. The open caseback reveals the decorated automatic movement.

name: Captain Cook Automatic
description: The Rado Captain Cook revives the brand's 1962 dive watch with a faithful vintage-inspired design featuring a compressor-style case and box sapphire crystal. Reintroduced in 2017, it quickly became Rado's most popular model. The 42mm stainless steel case with ceramic bezel insert houses a Powermatic 80 movement, delivering authentic dive watch heritage with modern reliability.

name: HyperChrome Automatic
description: The Rado HyperChrome combines sporty aesthetics with Rado's high-tech ceramic expertise. The monobloc ceramic case is machined from a single piece of plasma high-tech ceramic, making it virtually scratch-proof. The sweeping lines and integrated design create a modern sport watch that is lighter than steel and more durable than most conventional materials.

name: True Thinline
description: The Rado True Thinline pushes high-tech ceramic to its limits in an ultra-slim case measuring under 5mm thick. This quartz-powered model demonstrates that high-tech ceramic can be crafted with extraordinary precision and finesse. The minimalist dial and impossibly thin profile make it a statement piece that challenges conventional watch design proportions.

name: DiaMaster
description: The Rado DiaMaster represents the brand's most refined dress watch collection, featuring slim cases with plasma high-tech ceramic construction. The name derives from Rado's heritage of using ultra-hard materials, starting with diamonds in the DiaStar line. This model offers sophisticated aesthetics with Rado's signature scratch-resistant ceramic technology.

specs.caseMaterial: Plasma high-tech ceramic and stainless steel

name: Centrix Automatic
description: The Rado Centrix is a contemporary design-forward watch that pairs high-tech ceramic with stainless steel for a distinctive two-tone aesthetic. The integrated bracelet design and slim case create a comfortable, everyday-wearable timepiece. The Centrix collection has become a gateway to the Rado brand, offering the signature ceramic experience at an accessible price point.

name: Captain Cook High-Tech Ceramic
description: The Rado Captain Cook High-Tech Ceramic takes the popular Captain Cook dive watch and crafts it entirely in Rado's signature high-tech ceramic. The matte green ceramic case and bracelet are virtually scratch-proof while maintaining 300m water resistance. This model represents the ultimate expression of Rado's material innovation applied to a dive watch format.

name: True Square Open Heart
description: The Rado True Square Open Heart combines the brand's ceramic expertise with the romance of an open-worked dial. The aperture at 12 o'clock reveals the balance wheel and escapement, offering a window into the mechanical heart of the watch. The high-tech ceramic case ensures this artistic expression is protected by one of the hardest materials in watchmaking.

name: HyperChrome Chronograph
description: The Rado HyperChrome Chronograph adds a sporty chronograph complication to the high-tech ceramic HyperChrome line. The automatic chronograph movement powers three sub-dials within a plasma ceramic case that is virtually scratch-proof. Its 44.9mm case gives it a commanding presence on the wrist while remaining lighter than an equivalent steel chronograph.

name: DiaStar Original
description: The Rado DiaStar Original is a faithful reissue of the groundbreaking 1962 DiaStar, the world's first scratch-resistant watch. The original DiaStar's hardmetal case and sapphire crystal were revolutionary, and this modern version maintains the distinctive oval case and integrated bracelet. It remains a design icon and a testament to Rado's pioneering spirit in materials innovation.

name: Classics Index Automatic
description: The Frederique Constant Classics Index is the brand's entry point to Swiss automatic watchmaking, offering an elegant dress watch at an accessible price. Founded in 1988 with the mission to make luxury Swiss watches affordable, Frederique Constant delivers on that promise with this model. The FC-303 calibre provides reliable timekeeping in a traditionally styled 40mm case.

name: Highlife Automatic COSC
description: The Frederique Constant Highlife Automatic COSC features a COSC-certified chronometer movement in a sporty integrated bracelet design. The Highlife collection, inspired by the 1999 original, features a distinctive six-screw bezel and interchangeable strap system. This model offers chronometric precision and versatile styling at a price that undercuts most Swiss COSC-certified competitors.

name: Slimline Moonphase Manufacture
description: The Frederique Constant Slimline Moonphase features an in-house manufacture calibre with a beautifully rendered moonphase display. The FC-705 is Frederique Constant's own movement, not based on an ETA or Sellita ebauche, making this an exceptional value for a manufacture moonphase. The slim profile and classic styling create an elegant dress watch.

name: Classic Worldtimer Manufacture
description: The Frederique Constant Classic Worldtimer features an in-house manufacture calibre that displays all 24 time zones simultaneously via a rotating city disc. The worldtimer complication is one of the most practical for travelers and one of the most visually appealing in watchmaking. Frederique Constant offers this complication at a fraction of the price of competing manufacture worldtimers.

name: Classics Heart Beat
description: The Frederique Constant Classics Heart Beat was the model that put the brand on the horological map in 2004. The open-worked dial at 12 o'clock reveals the oscillating balance wheel, a feature Frederique Constant pioneered at an accessible price point. This 'Heart Beat' concept demonstrated that fine watchmaking could be appreciated visually without a premium price tag.

name: Highlife Perpetual Calendar Manufacture
description: The Frederique Constant Highlife Perpetual Calendar features an in-house manufacture calibre with a perpetual calendar that automatically accounts for months of varying lengths and leap years until 2100. Offering a manufacture perpetual calendar at under $10,000 is exceptional value in the Swiss watch industry, where such complications typically cost five to six figures.

name: Runabout Automatic
description: The Frederique Constant Runabout collection pays tribute to the elegant wooden runabout boats of the 1920s-1930s. The brand partners with the Riva Historical Society to celebrate this nautical heritage. The distinctive red minute track and vintage-inspired dial recall the mahogany hulls and brass fittings of classic speedboats, creating a uniquely themed timepiece.

name: Classics Premiere Automatic
description: The Frederique Constant Classics Premiere offers an entry into mechanical watchmaking with a refined, traditional design. The 38.5mm case size is well-suited to both dress and casual wear, while the FC-301 automatic calibre delivers reliable performance. Roman numeral indices and blued steel hands give it timeless classical appeal.

name: Horological Smartwatch
description: The Frederique Constant Horological Smartwatch was the first Swiss-made connected watch from a traditional manufacturer when it launched in 2015. It maintains the appearance of a classic Swiss timepiece while embedding activity tracking and sleep monitoring via a connected movement. No digital screen is present; all smart data is displayed via the analog hands and a companion app.

name: Manufacture Tourbillon
description: The Frederique Constant Manufacture Tourbillon houses an in-house manufactured tourbillon calibre, a complication traditionally reserved for watches costing tens of thousands of dollars. Frederique Constant's democratization of the tourbillon mirrors the brand's founding mission of making luxury watchmaking accessible. The FC-980 calibre features a flying tourbillon visible through the dial aperture at 6 o'clock.

specs.caseMaterial: Stainless steel with rose gold plating

name: Precisionist Chronograph
description: The Bulova Precisionist uses a proprietary three-prong quartz crystal vibrating at 262.144 kHz, making it 8x more precise than standard quartz watches with accuracy to within 10 seconds per year. The continuously sweeping second hand (not stepping) mimics a mechanical movement's sweep. The Precisionist technology represents Bulova's continued innovation in quartz timekeeping.

name: Lunar Pilot Chronograph
description: The Bulova Lunar Pilot is a faithful re-creation of the chronograph worn by astronaut Dave Scott on the Apollo 15 mission in 1971, making it the first privately owned watch worn on the Moon. When Scott's NASA-issued Omega Speedmaster crystal popped off during EVA, he wore his personal Bulova on the lunar surface. This model uses the high-frequency 262 kHz Precisionist movement.

specs.strapMaterial: Black leather strap with nylon NATO included

name: Curv Chronograph
description: The Bulova Curv features the world's first curved chronograph movement, designed to follow the natural contour of the wrist. The high-performance quartz movement vibrates at 262 kHz for exceptional accuracy with a smooth, continuous sweep. The distinctive curved case profile sets it apart from every other chronograph on the market and showcases Bulova's engineering prowess.

name: Marine Star Chronograph
description: The Bulova Marine Star is a sport-oriented chronograph with 100m water resistance and a bold design featuring silicone accents on the stainless steel bracelet. The collection has been a staple of Bulova's lineup for decades, offering reliable quartz chronograph functionality at an accessible price. It is one of the brand's best-selling sport watch lines.

specs.strapMaterial: Stainless steel bracelet with blue silicone accents

name: Sutton Automatic
description: The Bulova Sutton Automatic is a refined dress watch featuring a skeleton dial that reveals the Miyota 8N24 automatic movement. The open-worked dial and exhibition caseback allow appreciation of the mechanical movement from both sides. Named after the historic Sutton Place in New York City, it reflects Bulova's heritage as a quintessentially American watch brand founded in 1875.

name: Classic Automatic
description: The Bulova Classic Automatic offers an affordable entry into mechanical watchmaking with a clean, versatile design. The open caseback reveals the Miyota 8205 automatic movement, while the silver dial with applied indices creates a refined look. Bulova's Classic collection embodies understated elegance that makes these watches suitable for both business and casual settings.

name: Hack Watch
description: The Bulova Hack Watch is a reissue of the iconic A-11 military watch that Bulova produced for the U.S. Army during World War II. The A-11 specification, issued to multiple manufacturers, became the template for all modern field watches. This faithful recreation features a 38mm case with a hacking mechanical movement, and its military dial with radium-style lume preserves the original's character.

name: Archive Series Computron
description: The Bulova Computron revives the trapezoidal LED watch that Bulova produced in the 1970s as a competitor to the Hamilton Pulsar. The original Computron was one of the most distinctive LED watches of the era with its angular, futuristic case design. This modern reissue uses an LED display that activates on button press, faithfully recreating the experience of the original space-age timepiece.

name: Oceanographer Snorkel
description: The Bulova Oceanographer Snorkel revives the brand's 1970s dive watch with its distinctive internal rotating bezel operated by a secondary crown at 4 o'clock. The original Devil Diver/Snorkel was one of Bulova's most popular sport watches. This reissue features a 44mm case with automatic movement and 200m water resistance, bringing vintage dive watch charm to a modern platform.

name: Devil Diver
description: The Bulova Devil Diver is a reissue of the iconic 1960s dive watch that earned its name from the devil mascot on the original's caseback. This heritage model combines vintage aesthetics with modern specifications, including an automatic movement and 200m water resistance. The cushion-shaped case and bold dial design recall the adventurous spirit of the original deep-sea timepiece.

name: Altiplano Ultimate Automatic
description: The Piaget Altiplano Ultimate Automatic is among the thinnest automatic watches in the world at just 4.30mm. Piaget has been the master of ultra-thin watchmaking since 1957 when it created the Calibre 9P, and the Altiplano Ultimate continues this legacy with the in-house 910P calibre. The movement uses the caseback as the mainplate, achieving an extraordinary level of thinness.

name: Polo S
description: The Piaget Polo S marked Piaget's entry into the luxury steel sport watch segment when it launched in 2016. With its cushion-shaped case, integrated bracelet, and in-house automatic calibre, it represents Piaget's answer to the Royal Oak and Nautilus. The horizontally textured dial and refined finishing demonstrate that Piaget's haute horlogerie expertise extends to sporty territory.

name: Limelight Gala
description: The Piaget Limelight Gala is a jewellery watch embodying Piaget's dual expertise in haute horlogerie and haute joaillerie. The asymmetric case design with its distinctive curved lines has been a Piaget icon since 1973. Set with brilliant-cut diamonds on the bezel, this model showcases Piaget's gem-setting mastery that has made it a favorite of royalty and Hollywood stars.

name: Altiplano
description: The Piaget Altiplano is the quintessential ultra-thin dress watch, embodying the brand's mastery of slim watchmaking since the original 1957 Calibre 9P. This 40mm model with the hand-wound 430P calibre measures just 6.36mm thick, delivering extraordinary elegance on the wrist. The Altiplano has become synonymous with understated luxury and is often considered the finest ultra-thin dress watch available.

name: Polo Date
description: The Piaget Polo Date is the evolution of the Polo S, refining the sport-chic formula with a slightly restyled case and dial. The integrated bracelet design and slim profile distinguish it from bulkier luxury sport watches. Powered by Piaget's in-house 1110P calibre, it continues the brand's tradition of creating elegant timepieces that work from boardroom to beach.

name: Possession
description: The Piaget Possession features the collection's iconic turning bezel, inspired by the Possession ring collection introduced in 1990. The rotating bezel is both a tactile experience and a design statement, set with a single diamond at 12 o'clock. This ladies' watch combines Piaget's jewelry expertise with Swiss watchmaking in a playful, elegant package.

name: Altiplano Ultimate Concept
description: The Piaget Altiplano Ultimate Concept is the thinnest mechanical watch in the world at just 2mm thick, a record-breaking achievement unveiled in 2018. The entire watch IS the movement: the caseback serves as the mainplate, and five patents were filed for this revolutionary architecture. Cobalt alloy construction and a new barrel design made this engineering marvel possible.

name: Piaget Polo Skeleton
description: The Piaget Polo Skeleton combines the sporty Polo case with an open-worked movement that reveals the intricate architecture of the in-house 1200S calibre. The skeleton work is executed with Piaget's renowned decorative finishing, with every bridge and plate hand-decorated. At just 6.5mm thick, it is one of the thinnest skeletonized sport watches available.

name: Emperador Coussin 1270P
description: The Piaget Emperador Coussin houses the ultra-thin Calibre 1270P, one of the world's thinnest automatic tourbillon movements at 5.55mm. The cushion-shaped case design provides the space needed for this complex complication while maintaining Piaget's commitment to slim proportions. The flying tourbillon at 10 o'clock is visible through a dial aperture, showcasing Piaget's haute horlogerie mastery.

name: Dancer
description: The Piaget Dancer is a classic round dress watch that epitomizes understated haute horlogerie elegance. The thin 18k white gold case houses a quartz movement, allowing for an exceptionally slim profile. The Dancer collection has been part of Piaget's lineup since the 1980s, offering a refined, jewelry-focused design that appeals to those who value discretion and quality materials.
